title: Instrumentality and Altered States film
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality and Altered States (film)             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Jul 27, 2010 11:45 pm I recently watched the 1980 film "Altered States." I highly reccomend you watch the film, but to give you a rough view, it is about a man who uses a drug and a sensory deprivation chamber to attempt to reach the base of human consciousness and existence. In the end, however, he realizes that this is an empty goal that leads to nothingness, and it reminded me A LOT of the concept of Instrumentality in EoE. In fact, the main character from the film actually becomes a blobous creature when he reaches the base of existence, much like humans becoming LCL. I believe that Anno may have even been slightly influenced by this concept, as it is remarkably similar. Thoughts? PS: This is much easier if you watch the film. It is available to stream from Netflix.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Jul 28, 2010 3:03 am I saw the film a few years back. Horrible ending, which is a shame because the rest of the movie was pretty enjoyable. It's not just the sensory deprevation that he trys ether, there were mind altering drugs involved in his research. I didn't see the connection when I saw the movie, namely because he's the only one going trough this evolutionary calandrum, Instrumentality was a gloval effect. But the concept remains, and some of the more philosophycal elements do parallel on occation but I think you might be reaching.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Jul 28, 2010 3:09 am berto wrote: I saw the film a few years back. Horrible ending, which is a shame because the rest of the movie was pretty enjoyable. It's not just the sensory deprevation that he trys ether, there were mind altering drugs involved in his research. I didn't see the connection when I saw the movie, namely because he's the only one going trough this evolutionary calandrum, Instrumentality was a gloval effect. But the concept remains, and some of the more philosophycal elements do parallel on occation but I think you might be reaching.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 28, 2010 10:38 am There is a connection there though, as what is an eva but a giant sensory deprivation tank for it's resident soul? Put that way it's no wonder why Yui seems batshit crazy whenever we get a glimpse behind the curtain... and her lucidity at the end of EoE seems unbelievable unless she had an instrumentality experience as well to sort her out.
